name: OpenAI Team
description: High-signal OpenAI launches verified against official OpenAI announcements and Help Center release notes.
headline: Verified OpenAI launches across {days} days.
subtitle: January 7 — April 9, 2026. Company-level launches from official OpenAI sources.
footnote: Cross-checked on April 13, 2026 against OpenAI announcement pages and OpenAI Help Center release notes.

releases.title: ChatGPT Health
releases.description: OpenAI introduced a dedicated Health space in ChatGPT with purpose-built encryption, isolated memory, and conversation siloing — health data not used to train models or shared with other chats.

releases.title: ChatGPT Go tier and ads
releases.description: OpenAI launched the $8/month ChatGPT Go plan globally and announced ad testing for Free and Go users in the US — conversation-relevant ads appearing below answers, clearly labeled. Paid tiers remain ad-free.

releases.title: More visual ChatGPT responses
releases.description: Responses for everyday questions gained at-a-glance visuals (stats, unit conversions, calculations) and tap-to-expand highlights for key people, places, and products with sourced side panels. Available on iOS, Android, and web.

releases.title: Codex app for macOS
releases.description: OpenAI launched the Codex desktop app as a command center for supervising multiple long-running agents in parallel. Available to Plus, Pro, Business, Enterprise, and Edu plans on Apple Silicon (macOS 14+).

releases.title: GPT-5.3-Codex
releases.description: OpenAI released GPT-5.3-Codex as its most capable agentic coding model, combining coding, reasoning, and computer-use strength. First OpenAI model rated "High" cybersecurity capability under its Preparedness Framework.

releases.title: Ads go live in ChatGPT Free and Go
releases.description: Advertising officially rolled out in ChatGPT for Free and Go tier users in the US, following the January announcement period.

releases.title: GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark
releases.description: Ultra-fast real-time coding model running on Cerebras Wafer Scale Engine 3 at 1000+ tokens/second. ChatGPT Pro research preview — first OpenAI model on dedicated Cerebras low-latency hardware.

releases.title: Projects can add sources from apps and chats
releases.description: ChatGPT Projects gained app links, saved chat outputs, and ad-hoc text as reusable, always-in-context project sources.

releases.title: GPT-5.4
releases.description: New mainline flagship across ChatGPT, Codex, and API: 1M token context, Codex-level coding, 75% on OSWorld computer use (exceeding human baseline at 72.4%), and 33% fewer per-claim errors vs GPT-5.2.

releases.title: GPT-5.4 mini and nano
releases.description: Faster, cheaper variants: mini (400k context, $0.75/$4.50 per MTok) and nano (API only, $0.20/$1.25 per MTok). Free-tier ChatGPT upgraded to GPT-5.4 mini; mini available in Codex for subagent parallelism.

releases.title: File Library in ChatGPT
releases.description: ChatGPT began saving uploaded and model-generated files into a persistent File Library, reusable across chats without re-uploading.

releases.title: ChatGPT on Apple CarPlay
releases.description: OpenAI launched ChatGPT in Apple CarPlay, enabling fully hands-free voice conversations for drivers on iOS 26.4 or later with a supported car.

releases.title: New $100/month ChatGPT Pro tier
releases.description: OpenAI inserted a new $100/month tier between Plus and the $200 Pro plan, offering 5× Codex usage vs Plus and the full model suite, with a launch promo of 10× through May 31.
